title: choretrum candollei
intro: Choretrum candollei, also known as the 'New Zealand pepper tree,' is a plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its fragrant foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Choretrum candollei include relief from congestion, coughs, and sore throats, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Choretrum candollei typically involve infusions or tinctures of the leaves, which can be consumed as a tea or added to remedies to alleviate respiratory issues.
